sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery is the primary treatment for localized vulvar cancer (SCC, adenocarcinoma, and melanoma) with the goal of achieving negative margins while preserving function and minimizing morbidity.

cards.name: Simple partial vulvectomy
cards.condition: Stage IA SCC/adenocarcinoma with ≤1 mm invasion
cards.contraindications: Invasion >1 mm, higher stage, or multifocal disease
cards.detail: Local excision with 1- to 2-cm margins. Key complications: wound infection, dehiscence, pain. Operative mortality: low (<1%).

cards.name: Radical partial vulvectomy
cards.condition: Stage IB or select Stage II SCC/adenocarcinoma
cards.contraindications: Stage IA disease, extensive involvement requiring total vulvectomy, or tumor not resectable with negative margins without removing proximal urethra, bladder, or anus
cards.detail: Excision of tumor with wide margins and deep tissue to fascia. Key complications: wound complications, lymphedema, sexual dysfunction. Operative mortality: low (<1%).

cards.name: Radical total vulvectomy
cards.contraindications: Localized tumor amenable to partial vulvectomy, or involvement of proximal urethra, bladder, or anus requiring pelvic exenteration
cards.detail: Excision of entire vulva with wide margins. Key complications: significant wound morbidity, lymphedema, psychosexual impact. Operative mortality: low (<1%).

cards.name: Inguinofemoral lymphadenectomy
cards.condition: Clinically node-positive disease or SLNB positive with macrometastases
cards.contraindications: Clinically node-negative disease where SLNB is appropriate, or high surgical risk unacceptably elevating complication rates
cards.detail: Removal of superficial inguinal and deep femoral lymph nodes via separate incision. Key complications: lymphedema (30%–70%), wound complications (20%–40%), infection. Operative mortality: low (<1%).

cards.name: Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB)
cards.contraindications: Clinically node-positive, multifocal tumor, tumor ≥4 cm, or inability to use dual tracer technique
cards.detail: Identification and removal of sentinel nodes using radiocolloid and blue dye. Key complications: false-negative rate (6.4%), lymphedema (lower than lymphadenectomy). Operative mortality: minimal.

cards.name: Pelvic exenteration
cards.contraindications: Distant metastases, extensive pelvic sidewall involvement, or disease amenable to less radical resection
cards.detail: Radical resection of pelvic organs. Key complications: high morbidity, urinary diversion, colostomy. Operative mortality: higher (3%–5%).
